findings.title: Worker discovery is untested
findings.impact: The new command depends on find_worker_pid to map the requested worker address to the right local process, but every run-path test mocks this method out. Regressions in pgrep invocation, dscli self-filtering, empty output handling, or multi-line process output would not fail any test even though they make dscli logs select the wrong log or fall back to stale defaults.
findings.fix: Add focused unit tests for find_worker_pid by mocking subprocess.check_output, covering a matching worker line, dscli self-match filtering, no-match CalledProcessError, timeout, and multiple pgrep rows.

findings.title: Tail failure path has no regression test
findings.impact: If tail cannot read the resolved log file, run should return BaseCommand.FAILURE and report the stderr. The current tests only cover successful tail output and validation failures before tail runs, so a change that ignores tail errors or still returns success would pass.
findings.fix: Add a run-path test with subprocess.run returning a non-zero returncode and stderr, then assert the command returns BaseCommand.FAILURE and does not print successful log output.
